lede: A look at June 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Zinedine Zidane and Alan Turing, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: United Kingdom
events.desc: The United Kingdom votes in a referendum to leave the European Union, by 52% to 48%.

events.title: Ashton Eaton
events.desc: Ashton Eaton breaks the decathlon world record at the United States Olympic Trials.

events.title: Reddit
events.desc: American social news and discussion site Reddit is founded in Medford, Massachusetts by Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian.

events.title: 1946 Vancouver Island earthquake
events.desc: The 1946 Vancouver Island earthquake strikes Vancouver Island, British Columbia, Canada.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Germany's latest fighter aircraft, a Focke-Wulf Fw 190, is captured intact when it mistakenly lands at RAF Pembrey in Wales.

events.title: College Board
events.desc: The College Board administers the first SAT exam.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: At Fort Towson in the Oklahoma Territory, Confederate Brigadier General Stand Watie surrenders the last significant Confederate army.

events.title: Flanders
events.desc: A peace treaty between the Flemish and the French is signed at Athis-sur-Orge.

events.title: Reconquista
events.desc: The Spanish Reconquista: In the Battle of Moclín the Emirate of Granada ambush a superior pursuing force, killing most of them in a military disaster for the Kingdom of Castile.

events.title: War of Saint Sabas
events.desc: War of Saint Sabas: In the Battle of Trapani, the Venetians defeat a larger Genoese fleet, capturing all its ships.

births.name: Clarence Thomas
births.line: American lawyer and jurist, Associate Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States

births.name: Martti Ahtisaari
births.line: Finnish captain and politician, 10th President of Finland, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: John McAfee, British-American computer programmer and businessman, founded McAfee
Werner Best, German police officer and jurist
Boris Vian, French author, poet, and playwright
Henry Probus, duke of Wrocław and high duke of Kraków (born c. 1258)
